nodes.goal: Load Back Bay OSM layers (surface, parks, buildings) from a local PBF.
Fan the loaded layers out to the baseline render and the modified compute branch.
Baseline — Back Bay buildings coloured by their original OSM height (no compute on this branch).
Apply the what-if multiplier on the GPU: footprint area > 200 m^2 → height * 3, written to height_mod.
Modified — buildings coloured by the GPU-computed height_mod.

dataflow.name: Autark what-if shadow study
dataflow.description: Criterion-driven what-if for Boston's Back Bay split into a modular autk-grammar chain: a shared data node loads the PBF once and fans out through a data-pool to (a) a baseline map rendered directly from the loaded layers (no compute) and (b) a GPU compute node that flags buildings with footprint > 200 m^2 and writes height_mod, which a second map renders side-by-side with the baseline.
